transcripts.title: IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology

segments.text:
Hello, everyone,
 I am Jack, your IELTS teacher.

And welcome back to another
 episode of the IELTS Plus English podcast.

We are going through a period
 of really fun topics for me,

this, as I told you before,
 this is a 40 topic curriculum.

And of course, there are good topics and
 bad topics, hard topics and easy topics.

But for me right now,
 this is a real golden period.

We did society - very interesting, 
sport - something I do a lot.

And today we're doing technology,
 which is something I am so interested

in, and it's the thing
 I love to spend my money on. You know,

some people might spend their money
 on clothing, on food, on experiences.
I love technology.
So looking forward to today's topic.

Remember, I do have ten vocabulary
 expressions that goes with this podcast.

You can find that at 
www.jacks-english.com

And if you're in Korea
 and you're familiar with Naver,
you can go to my Naver Cafe Jack's English

영국 영어, and you'll find everything
 there that you'll need
for all of the podcast episodes so far.

To start off today's episode,
 I want to think about

two very interesting words
 which are on today's word list

because it divides people into two groups
 those that are very interested

and comfortable with technology
 and those who are not.

Perhaps they're scared of technology,
 or they just don't like technology.

And the first group
 are called technophiles.
The second group are called technophobes.
I'm a technophile, a technophile.

That means that I'm
 really interested in technology.

Technophobe is someone who doesn't like
 it, doesn't really want to use technology.
So how about you? What are you?

Are you a technophile or a technophobe
 if you're listening to the podcast?
Think about your answer.

If you are on YouTube,
 then let me know in the comments below.
Let's start with some IELTS

part one questions to get me warmed up.
 I certainly need it.
So our first question today is:
Do you enjoy using technology?
Yes, I really enjoy using technology.

I use it in, I think,
 all aspects of my life.

Whether it's work,
 I'm using my laptop, my phone
equipment for my podcast or sports.
I like to look at my phone to analyse

whatever sport I'm doing
 or at home with my entertainment.

So technology
 is a huge part of my life.
OK, so let's do another one.
What gadgets do you use on a daily basis?

I have a number of
 gadgets I use on a daily basis.

The ones that come to my mind
 immediately are my phone, my laptop,
my e-book reader, my camera.

But the one that's really sticking out
 for me this year is my e-book reader,
which is quite a new piece of technology
that I use on a day to day basis.
OK, I'm warmed up.

Let's do a part two,
 and this looks pretty comfortable.
Describe an important gadget in your life.

So I'm just going to talk about the thing
 that comes to my mind first,
and I think I can talk for a long time.
So two minutes starts now.
I'd like to tell you about my phone.
Just last year, I got a new phone.

I've always had kind of okay phones,
 but last year I decided to upgrade

to kind of a top
 of the range. Apple Phone,
which had... it is the new iPhone 13,

and it has all the new technology,
 the good camera, etc.
And I was very excited

because I like photography and videography
 so I was excited to have this

that I can carry around
 with me day to day.
And it didn't disappoint.
I was really impressed.

I still am really impressed
 by how good the camera

is, how quick the phone is,
 how easy it is to use.
You know, it's quite

an intuitive design,
 so it's quite easy to learn how to use it,

contrary to what I was told - a lot of
 people said Apple phones are difficult.

So let me tell you
 a little more about my phone.

The important thing for me was the camera,
 because as I said, I do like

videography, particularly,
 I want to take good videos of my daughter

and I want to take photographs of the
 changing seasons, this kind of thing.

So that was really important for me when
 I made this decision to to buy the phone.
In my opinion, it's definitely the

best phone I've ever had,
 I've had several phones,

and I've liked some of them,
 I've always used Samsung.

But this is definitely
 the best phone I've ever had,

and I assume in the future
 I will stick with Apple,

but we'll see I'm
 not going to be very stuck to one brand.

In the future. I will continue
 using this for a long time.

I do like to use my phones
 for as long as possible.

I don't want to just keep switching
 every year or two. So I will...
OK, there you go.
Now I think that was OK.
I did try to use a few words in my list.

I was actually a little put off
 by some drilling sound in the background.

I don't know if you can hear that
 on the podcast. We will see.

But yeah, someone is doing
 some building work

in this building, so it's a little bit
 put off by that, but no excuses.
I was quite happy with that answer.
Let's do some part three.

What kind of technological developments
 have made the biggest impact on our lives?

I think the the biggest
 technological development that comes to

my mind is the phone, the smart phone,
 because it was a real game changer.

The reason I say it's a game
 changer is because of the smart phone.
A lot of other technology became obsolete.

For example, the MP three player,
 the digital camera,

there's several things
 that we used to need.

The other one is a satellite
 navigation in our cars.
We used to need these.
But nowadays the smart phones

have made them obsolete,
 meaning we don't need them anymore.
So I would say that the smart phone

is the best example of technology
 that's changed our lives.
Good. OK. Pretty good answer.

I think I would like to have an example
 in there, like personal to me.

So, you know, if you're thinking about
lengthening your answer in part three,

really try to think about, are you giving
 your examples. That can be very powerful.
I'll try to do that in the next one.

So the next question is, do you think
 people rely too much on modern technology?
Yes, I think so.

I'm not sure it's such a big deal,
 but yes, I think people
rely a lot on modern technology.

For example, people
 rely on modern technology for writing
auto correct feature.

So I think a lot of people's writing
 skill is going down.
You know, there's not so many good,

naturally good writers or people
 that have practised writing a lot.

Another one that comes to
 my mind is our reliance on maps

has made people very bad with direction
 and reading maps.

And when I say reliance on maps, I'm
 of course talking about our
Google Maps on our phones.
So perhaps people's sense of direction,

finding our way,
 that's getting worse nowadays.
Good. Let's do one more.

How effective is the use of
 computers in the classroom?
I think computers can be a good thing,
but I'm not convinced.

I'm not convinced
 how useful they are yet, because
let's talk about technology as a whole.
I find that phones are a distraction.

I find that iPads are useful
 in the classroom,
but you've got to use them well.

You've got to have a very specific reason
 for using them in the classroom.

The thing that I would be interested
 to look into as a teacher
would be using more computer systems
in my academy to keep student profiles

and to show their progress and graphs
 and these kind of things that,

for me, a very interesting
 kind of performance analysis.
Now I'm going very deep there,

but for me, as a teacher,
 that would be very interesting.

OK, good. That last answer was not really me
 trying to follow

an IELTS structure;
 that was actually my genuine thought.

But of course, in the IELTS exam,
 if there's something you care about
and you're really interested in, use it.

You know, talk about the things
 that you're interested in

because that can allow you to provide
 a lot of language to the examiner.
Good. OK. I think we'll leave it there.

Another nice topic, and I will be back
 next week with the next episode.
So have a wonderful week.
Study hard review today.

Preview the next topic,
 and I'll be back very soon.
See you next Wednesday. Bye bye.

way to answer this type of question how to make 
your opinion extra clear and develop your ideas

that's what the examiners look for there's also a 
PDF with the essay for you to download it's Asiya

here let's get started [Music] I've selected quite 
a challenging topic that was used in real exams

some people believe the government spends too much 
money on Space research while there are still a

lot of problems on the earth to what extent do you 
agree or disagree you know the rest right write it

at least 250 words and include relevant examples 
from your own experience or knowledge so first of

all look at the question to what extent do you 
agree or disagree so this is an opinion essay

which means that the whole essay is about what 
you think and that's where it gets interesting

what is the examiner looking for to put it simply 
your ability to maintain a good point of view

backed by good arguments and relevant examples 
so what opinion can you have there are three

options to agree with this statement arguing 
that indeed too much money is spent and that

it would be better to spend it on other things 
or you can disagree with the statement and argue

that even though there are pressing problems on 
Earth this is still money well spent or you can

have a balanced opinion or a position somewhere 
in the middle and argue for and against in real

life situations there are more often than not 
two sides to a story and if I'm being honest I

should take option three but this is not real life 
arguing on both sides will complicate the essay

it's easier to take either option one or two in 
our case so think about both which one is easier

to argue pick it and it doesn't really matter 
what you think today we could actually choose

any of the three but some topics are presented 
in such a way that it's difficult to choose one

particular side because you are directed towards 
another answer so you should really learn how to

argue all three sides that's what we do in my 
courses but today for Simplicity I'm going to

argue the side which I think is easier I disagree 
basically I will argue that the government should

continue to spend money on space exploration to 
make this answer better I asked Chad for some

examples so I'd like to show them to you because 
if you get a similar Topic in your exam you can

use them I have two main arguments well firstly we 
rely on Space Technologies in our everyday lives

think satellites we use them for communication 
the internet and satellite TV navigation GPS

in your car planes ships and observation of our 
planet wether forecasting and climate monitoring

we also turn space Technologies into everyday 
products memory phone mattresses small phone

cameras which were developed for using spacecraft 
robots invisible braces so that's what I'm wearing

and apparently they come from mapping the surface 
of Mars baby formula it was developed to provide

nutrition to astronauts and so on so we're 
not going to use all of them I just wanted

you to have some ideas in case you need them 
now let's have a closer look at each paragraph

the opening paragraph should be short and has 
two objectives the first is to rewrite the task

question statement in your own words According 
to some the millions spent annually on government

funded space projects would be better invested 
in addressing urgent issues on this planet the

second is to to take up a position or opinion 
although there are undoubtedly many problems

on Earth that need tackling I firmly believe 
that diverting financial resources from space

exploration would be a mistake these projects 
have brought and will continue to bring great

benefits to humanity three sentences remember from 
the start the examiner is looking at your grammar

you choice of vocabulary and how you connect your 
ideas together our sentences are complex in the

first notice the paraphrasing spend too much 
money on Space research becomes the millions

spent on government funded space projects would 
be better invested and then a lot of problems on

Earth is expressed as urgent issues on this planet 
the second sentence admits that there are problems

to be solved and there is a great collecation 
to tackle problems here many problems on Earth

that need tackling and then it goes on to State 
the position in this essay quite simply space

research is beneficial to humanity not spending 
money on it would be a mistake in this complex

sentence very important we need a comma examiners 
do check if you punctuate correctly so we have our

dependent clause starting with although at the 
beginning of the sentence which means we need

to put a comma after it and the last sentence 
already mentions the benefits which we're going

to discuss in our body paragraphs so there is 
a great linking between paragraphs it's a good

start the point of view is clear two sentences are 
well linked with although both both the vocabulary

and grammar are quite impressive what paragraph 
one if we have an opinion in the essay we have

to justify it and that's all this paragraph does 
and does it in the simplest most straightforward

way keep things simple in your essay that's a 
general advice so we're going to start with the

topic sentence and then give some examples and 
make any relevant comments the first sentence

Carries On from the opinion in the introduction 
which was these projects have brought and will

continue to bring great benefits to humanity 
and we begin an outstanding example of this

is the remarkable improvement in our lives due to 
the development of satellite technology a direct

result of space exploration we are saying that 
the satellite technology has changed our lives

on Earth for the better that's our topic sentence 
which means that the whole paragraph will be about

the satellites now we present some examples over 
several Generations satellites orbiting the Earth

have not only facilitated scientific advances in 
areas such as weather monitoring and navigation

but have also transformed our daily lives first we 
give examples of more General improvements by Le

navigation and then we mention our daily lives 
in the next sentence we can continue talking

about our daily lives and our sentences will 
be perfectly connected thanks to satellites we

have witnessed profound changes in the way we 
connect with each other using the internet to

bridge distances and accessing international news 
and TV broadcasts with unprecedented convenience

so we give more examples and now we just need to 
wrap up this body paragraph satellite technology

has interconnected the world shaping the way we 
live today so this sentence summarizes what this

paragraph was about the writing style of the essay 
is formal have a look at some of the adjectives we

used an outstanding example remarkable Improvement 
profound changes we also have a great grammatical

structure not only but also satellites orbiting 
the Earth have not only facilitated scientific

advances but have also transformed our daily 
lives I know it's quite complex to make it

simpler we could say satellites have not only 
helped scientists make progress but have also

changed our daily lives good go with what you 
feel comfortable with now let's have a look at

the next sentence it uses present participles 
using accessing let me show you how to write a

sentence like this we begin with a few simple 
sentences because of satellites we have seen

big changes in how we connect with each other we 
use the internet to reach people far away and we

watch news and TV from around the world look 
at the subjects and verbs we have seen we use

we watch we have the same subject we which means 
we can link all these sentences into one because

of satellites we have seen big changes in how we 
connect with each other comma using the internet

to reach people far away and easily watching 
news and TV from around the world now this is

is a highly complex sentence and going back to 
our body paragraph the verb tense is mainly the

present perfect to show how developments which 
began some time ago continue to have effect on

us today and one more comment here notice that the 
examples go into some detail but not too much and

also there is no mention of any particular 
country or organization I'm not saying you

shouldn't include more local or specific examples 
but be careful don't go into too much detail what

do you think about this example the space research 
program in India has been very successful and has

encouraged young people to take up Scientific 
careers great example but be careful not to

include too much local detail and numerical 
data like here there has been a 25% rise in

University applications in airspace engineering 
since the space program was launched that's not

a good example The Examiner won't be interested 
to read details like this now let's have a look

at the second body paragraph furthermore carton 
AG Technologies initially developed for space

exploration have been successfully adapted and 
turned into every typ products we use on Earth the

opening sentence introduces the second idea space 
Technologies are turned into everyday products

which is good to link the two body paragraphs are 
used for the more you could also use moreover then

these spinoffs like a spin-off is a useful result 
of an activity designed to produce something else

these spinoffs cover a wide range from comfortable 
memory phone mattresses to Tiny cameras in Mobile

phones which were developed from efforts to make 
cameras smaller for using spacecraft here again

we have a number of examples not every essay 
will have that many but now we need a bit of

explanation to tie the examples back to the 
original essay topic these examples illustrate

how Innovations originally intended for space 
exploration continue to yield practical benefits

to humankind to Y is to do something good to bring 
benefits and results looking at the paragraph We

have some topic specific vocabulary which is 
great to have cuttingedge Technologies to mean

the latest most Advanced Technologies spin-offs 
and spacecraft now also pay attention to linking

words how many do we have oh basically only one 
forther more don't think that you need to have a

lot of linking words in every paragraph of your 
essay how did we link sentences instead by using

these spin-offs these examples to refer back 
to our previous ideas so don't think you need

a lot of linkers be careful to use referencing in 
your essay because examiners check if you do now

an essay must have a beginning a middle and an 
end I examiners say that test takers sometimes

write a great essay and then don't include 
a strong ending a conclusion that goes over

the main points made so it's important that 
you have enough time to write it here is the

first sentence there there is no doubt that the 
expenditure on Space research is enormous this

sentence refers back to our topic and repeats the 
fact that space research costs money we show that

we remember what the question was but it's also a 
great opportunity to show off more vocabulary more

synonyms uh the million spent in the troduction 
becomes expenditure is enormous next restate your

opinion yet upon examining the numerous ways 
Humanity has benefited from it I am convinced

that it is money well spent it begins with yet 
to contrast the huge investment and examples are

those mentioned in body paragraphs the positive 
impact spans all aspects of human life from Earth

monitoring and industrial production to improving 
the quality of of our life and revolutionizing our

leisure time I think what stands out here is the 
overall pattern how the essay never loses track

of what it's trying to do it's coherent it takes 
up the position and illustrates it and remember

that it's better to develop one main idea in each 
body paragraph It's where less is more don't try

to include too many ideas in my coures I show how 
to find ideas and transform them into a coherent

high scoring essay sentence by sentence and how 
to answer each type of task I'll link them in

the video description if you'd like to check them 
out and I actually have a video an in-depth video

on how to write a perfect introduction from 
my course on YouTube as a free preview and it

would be a great next step in your learning and 
don't forget to download our today's essay as PDF

thank you so much for watching me today good 
luck with your preparation and your exam bye
